title: Drift Negative Pattern Entry
description: Schema for labelled negative code patterns used to validate drift signal detection. Each entry describes a code pattern that drift should identify as problematic.

id.description: Unique identifier for this pattern. Must match the filename prefix (e.g. 'mutant_duplicate_001' for mutant_duplicate_001.py).

signal.description: The drift signal type this pattern targets. Must match a registered drift SignalType value.

origin.description: How this pattern was created. 'human' = manually written, 'ai_assisted' = human-guided AI output, 'ai_generated' = fully AI-generated code.

model_hint.description: Optional best-effort hint about which AI model produced this pattern (e.g. 'gpt-4o', 'claude-3.5-sonnet').

pattern_class.description: A short classification of the anti-pattern (e.g. 'copy_paste_with_variation', 'bare_except_pattern', 'missing_guard_clause').

confirmed_problematic.description: Whether this pattern has been manually confirmed as genuinely problematic (not just detected by drift).

description.description: Brief explanation of why this pattern is problematic.

tp_confirmed.description: Whether drift correctly detects this pattern (true positive confirmed by CI or manual verification).

added_by.description: Who added this pattern (e.g. 'bootstrap', a GitHub username, or 'community').

drift_version.description: The drift version against which this pattern was verified.
